title: Fix password gate routing, remove Trailer header, reorder press kit sections

requirements.summary: Move password gate to landing page to block access before navigating to press kit
acceptanceCriteria: When password protection is enabled, visiting '/' shows the PasswordGateModal on the landing page
The press kit page content is not visible while the password dialog is open
After entering the correct password, the user can access '/press-kit' and the modal does not reappear
The sessionStorage verification flag is set on successful password entry
If the user navigates directly to '/press-kit' without the sessionStorage flag set, they are redirected to the landing page where the password gate is shown

file_operations.description: Add PasswordGateModal to LandingPage that checks if password protection is enabled via useGetContent query. When passwordEnabled is true and sessionStorage verification flag is not set, show the modal. After successful verification, user can navigate to press kit. Import and use PasswordGateModal component with conditional rendering based on content.passwordEnabled and sessionStorage state.

Remove the PasswordGateModal from PressKitPage entirely. Add a useEffect hook that checks sessionStorage for the password verification flag when password protection is enabled. If the flag is missing, redirect the user to '/' using window.location.href or navigate. This ensures direct navigation to /press-kit without verification sends users back to the landing page where the gate will be shown.

Update PasswordGateModal to accept an optional onSuccess callback prop that can be used by LandingPage to handle post-verification navigation (e.g., navigating to /press-kit). Ensure the modal still sets the sessionStorage flag on successful password verification as it currently does.

requirements.summary: Remove the 'Trailer' header text from the video section on the press kit page
acceptanceCriteria: No text reading 'Trailer' (or any section heading for the video block) is visible on the press kit page
The video thumbnail and YouTube player still render and function correctly
No other content is removed or altered

file_operations.description:
Remove any heading or label text (e.g.,

Trailer

or similar) that appears above or around the VideoSection component in PressKitPage. Leave the VideoSection component itself intact so the video thumbnail and player continue to work. Only the section heading text should be deleted.

requirements.summary: Reorder press kit page sections to match the specified vertical sequence
acceptanceCriteria: The press kit page renders sections in exactly this order: Game logo → YouTube video → About → Game Details → Features → Screenshots → Download all screenshots → Socials → Press email
All section content remains unchanged — only their vertical order is modified
No sections are removed or duplicated
The single shared max-width container and alignment of all sections is preserved

file_operations.description: Reorder the JSX elements in PressKitPage to render sections in this exact vertical sequence: 1. Game logo image, 2. VideoSection (YouTube video), 3. About section, 4. Game Details section, 5. Features section, 6. ScreenshotsGallery, 7. MediaDownloadButton (Download all screenshots), 8. SocialMediaSection (Socials), 9. Press email section. Do not add, remove, or alter any content or styling within each section — only change the order in which they appear in the component's return statement.
